title: Symmetry-Guided Design of Quantum Couplers in Dirac materials: AA-Bilayer Graphene Coupler
abstract: We develop a theoretical framework for designing quantum couplers based on Dirac materials that can modulate the polarization of transmitted quasiparticles without significantly perturbing their propagation. We analyze in detail the conditions required for perfect transmission (Klein tunneling) together with controlled polarization transformation of the incoming states. We then discuss an explicit model of a quantum coupler composed of AA-stacked bilayer graphene nanoribbons with armchair edges and a localized interlayer interaction. Perfect transmission through the desired polarization channels is examined for both narrow and wide couplers. We show that the transmission of polarized states can be finely tuned by external fields.
summary: This paper presents a method to design quantum couplers in Dirac materials like graphene that can switch or rotate the polarization of particles. By utilizing the property of Klein tunneling, the authors show how to achieve perfect, lossless transmission. They demonstrate that external electric and magnetic fields can be used to precisely tune the output state, such as converting layer polarization to cone polarization. The framework is particularly effective for nanoribbon geometries where transport is restricted to specific channels.
main_problem: Designing a theoretical framework for quantum couplers in Dirac materials that can modulate the polarization of transmitted quasiparticles (such as layer, spin, or valley) without causing significant reflection or loss.
main_result: The authors developed a symmetry-guided design strategy that uses Klein tunneling to ensure reflectionless transmission, demonstrating that polarization can be precisely controlled via external electric and magnetic fields in AA-bilayer graphene.
method: The authors use an auxiliary Hamiltonian approach and a unitary transformation to 'reverse-engineer' the required potential for a desired polarization transformation, treating Klein tunneling as free-particle dynamics in disguise.
model_or_system: The system consists of AA-stacked bilayer graphene nanoribbons with armchair edges and localized interlayer interaction, described by a Hamiltonian including interlayer hopping, electric fields, and parallel magnetic fields.
key_observables: Polarization coefficients (layer, cone, and pseudo-spin), transmission amplitudes, reflection coefficients, and conductance.
important_parameters: Interlayer coupling strength (t), perpendicular electric field (v30), parallel magnetic field (v20), coupler length (L), and nanoribbon width (W).
main_assumptions: The construction is exact at normal incidence (py = 0) and assumes the continuity of the wavefunction at the coupler boundaries.
figures_summary: Figure 1 introduces the system; Figure 2 shows the dependence of polarization coefficients on the electric field; Figure 3 illustrates the oscillation of polarization coefficients as a function of other parameters.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a theoretical framework for coupler design, details the physical model of AA-bilayer graphene, applies a unitary transformation method to achieve controlled polarization, and analyzes various regimes including narrow-metallic and wider-coupler multi-channel transport.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: (Color online) The model of AA-stacked bilayer coupler. Two layers of graphene can get deformed such that they form the stripe of AA-stacking.
Figure 2: Polarization coefficients |c3±|2 (left) and |c1±|2 (right) in dependence on the electric field v30. We fixed |v10| = 0.1, n = 1 that corresponds to L ∼47.12.
Figure 3: Oscillation of polarization coefficients |c1±|2 and |c2±|2 as function of parallel magnetic field. We fixed |v10| = 0.1.

Figure 4: Transmission on the layer one |c3+|2 (left) and the layer two |c3−|2 (right) as a function of L and φ. The area between dashed curves has transmission greater than 95%. Black vertical lines symbolize the critical angle φ− c .

Figure 5: Transmission on the layer one |c3+|2 and the layer two |c3−|2 as a function of the energy E with v30 = 0 (upper line) and with v30 ̸= 0 (lower line). We set W = L = 6, t = 1, v30 = 3. The white line indicates E = p

Figure 6: The conductance of AA bilayer coupler with semiconducting AC edges . The parameter are v10 = t = 1, W = 1.03, EF = 3 and magic length L = L↓= 3/2π. The electric field is here denoted v30 ≡V0.
